bugs.title: conversation-meta question answered with the BTD6 data refusal
bugs.summary: "what is the last message you can see" → "I don't have verified BTD6 data… (55.1)". The reply legitimately quoted the prior Desperado turns; the guard treated those names as ungrounded and the floor copy is a non-sequitur for a non-BTD6 question.

bugs.title: conversation carryover unreachable: pronoun follow-ups never route BTD6
bugs.summary: "does the navarch of seas paragon make coin" answered correctly, then "does it make coins at the end of the round?" refused. Same shape: "which of those items can damage lead" after the Geraldo list.

bugs.title: BUG-0003 recurrence in the model loop: the tool laundered a misread quantity ($120,743,025)
bugs.summary: "how much do 10 041 despos cost on impop" → "$120,743,025" (= 10,041 × $12,025) despite the grounding line carrying the correct "×10 towers → Impoppable $120,250".

bugs.title: r-shorthand round projection answered with the wrong total (cumulative-from-round-1 mislabel)
bugs.summary: "How much do I have on r70 if I had 26932 at the end of r53" → "At the end of round 70, you would have a total of $71,315.20" (plus a correct $29,386.70 rounds-54-70 breakdown). $71,315.20 is the cumulative from round 1 through 70 — not the user's scenario. Truth: 26,932 + 29,38…

bugs.title: "despos" hallucinated as Plasma Monkey Fan Club (unguarded general path)
bugs.summary: "@SuperBot how much do 10 041 despos cost on impop" → "A single Plasma Monkey Fan Club (Despo) costs $54,000 on Impoppable. For 10,041 Despos: 10,041 × $54,000 = $542,214,000 (542.2 million)."

bugs.title: Elite boss HP answered with the Standard table labeled "Elite"
bugs.summary: "@SuperBot what is the hp of elite lych per tier" → "Elite Lych health per tier: Tier 1: 14,000 … Tier 5: 2,100,000" — those are the Standard Lych figures. Elite Lych is 30,000 / 180,000 / 1,100,000 / 4,800,000 / 24,000,000 (2-11× higher).

bugs.title: Round-cash question misrouted to the no-data denial
bugs.summary: user asked "@SuperBot lets say i have 8094$ at round 60, what is the cash that i will get by going to round 68" → bot replied "I don't have verified BTD6 data to answer that for the current game version (55.1). I won't state names or numbers I can't ground in my data — try askin…

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the AI Platform subsystem (cogs.ai_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for the AI Platform. When false, no AI feature runs for this guild — including BTD6 AI augmentation and the central natural-language stage.
Whether the bot may reply to natural-language messages (channel- and role-policy still apply). Independent of ai.enabled so admins can keep AI features available without enabling passive replies.

Default provider used by AI tasks that don't specify their own. 'deterministic' keeps responses local; 'openai' / 'anthropic' route through the configured OpenAI / Anthropic account. Leave the model empty so each task auto-picks the right model for the provider. This per-guild setting wins over the AI_DEFAULT_PROVIDER environment default for this server (see docs/ai-config-ownership.md § provider precedence).
Default model identifier for the chosen provider. Leave empty to use the provider's own default.
Default minimum XP level required to talk to the bot naturally. Channels, categories, and roles may override this in M2.
Seconds between natural-language replies per user. Zero disables the cooldown.
How many @-mention replies a fresh user (below the minimum level) is allowed before the level gate kicks in.

Free-text guild-wide instruction body that prefixes every AI prompt for this guild. Transitional scalar seed: M2 backfills this into a typed ai_instruction_profile row named 'default' and the typed table becomes the runtime source of truth.

How many minutes of recent channel messages the bot should keep in its in-process memory and include as context on each AI reply. ``0`` keeps only the last 3 messages per channel (the always-on minimum so basic conversational handles still work). Hard cap 120 minutes; the cache is in-process only and dropped on restart.

When ON, the bot may scan recent channel history via Discord's API to backfill its memory cache when the in-process buffer holds fewer messages than the configured window requires. OFF means memory is limited to messages the bot observed since process start.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Automod subsystem (cogs.automod_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for automod.  When off, no rule acts regardless of the per-rule toggles.  Off by default — a fresh server is unaffected.
Delete + warn on a burst of messages from one member in a channel.
Delete + warn on Discord invite links (discord.gg/…).
Delete + warn on messages that are mostly uppercase (shouting).
Delete + warn on messages with too many @mentions (mass-ping).
Delete + warn on a burst of messages from one member spread across MULTIPLE channels — the per-channel spam rule can't see this pattern since its window is scoped to one channel.
Delete + warn when the same message (any channel) repeats too many times — distinct from the spam rule, which is rate-only and doesn't look at what was actually said.
Messages from one member in one channel within the spam window before the spam rule trips.
Sliding-window length (seconds) the spam count is measured over.
Percentage of letters that must be uppercase before the caps rule trips (only on messages with enough letters to judge).
Number of @mentions in one message before the mass-mention rule trips.
Messages from one member across ANY channels within the spam window before the cross-channel spam rule trips.
Repeats of the same message (any channel) within the spam window before the duplicate-content rule trips.
Comma-separated role ids whose members are never acted on (staff, bots-with-a-role, …).  Leave empty for none.
Comma-separated channel ids automod never touches (announcements, memes, …).  Leave empty for none.

settings.purpose: BTD6 source-cache settings keys — **reserved, not yet wired** (M3B).
Settings keys owned by the Cleanup subsystem (cogs.cleanup_cog).

keys.hint: Window (seconds) the `!cleanuphistory` spam sweep treats two near-identical messages from one author as duplicates.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Counters subsystem (cogs.counters_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for server counters.  When off, no channel is renamed regardless of the bindings.  Off by default — a fresh server is unaffected.
Channel whose name shows the total member count (a locked voice channel works well).  Leave unset to disable this counter.
Channel whose name shows the non-bot member count.  Unset to disable.
Channel whose name shows the bot count.  Unset to disable.
Name template for the total counter.  Placeholder: {count}.
Name template for the humans counter.  Placeholder: {count}.
Name template for the bots counter.  Placeholder: {count}.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Economy subsystem (cogs.economy_cog).
Settings keys shared by game subsystems.

keys.hint: Default entry fee (🪙 coins) applied when an admin runs `!rpsregister` without an explicit entry_fee argument.
Default entry fee (🪙 coins) applied when an admin runs `!bjtournament` without an explicit entry_fee argument.
Seconds each player has to respond on their turn before the duel times out and the opponent wins by default.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the governance runtime (``governance/``).

keys.hint: Role whose members are treated as the 'trusted' tier — reserved for trust-gated features and surfaces.  Leave empty to disable.  Like the moderator role, this only ever adds standing.

Role whose members may use moderation actions (warn, timeout, kick, ban) without holding Discord moderation permissions.  Leave empty to require Discord permissions only.  This only adds access — no one who can moderate today loses it.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Image-moderation subsystem (cogs.image_moderation_cog).

keys.hint: Delete + warn on images flagged sexual / sexual-minors content.
Delete + warn on images flagged violent / graphic content.
Delete + warn on images flagged harassment content.
Delete + warn on images flagged hate content.
Confidence (percent) a category score must reach before an image is removed.  Higher = fewer false positives (80 recommended).
Comma-separated role ids whose members' images are never scanned (staff, trusted, …).  Leave empty for none.
Comma-separated channel ids image moderation never scans (art, memes, NSFW-gated, …).  Leave empty for none.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Karma subsystem (cogs.karma_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for karma. When off, the !thanks / !karma give commands politely decline. On by default.
How long a member must wait before thanking the same recipient again (seconds). The main anti-farm guard. 0 disables the cooldown.
Maximum karma grants one member can give per rolling 24 hours.
React-to-thank: set an emoji (e.g. ✨) and reacting with it on a message grants karma to that message's author — same cooldown and daily cap as !thanks. Leave empty to disable (the default).

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the server-logging service (services.server_logging).
Settings keys owned by the Moderation subsystem (cogs.moderation_cog).

keys.hint: Number of warnings before the escalation action is applied.  Set high to effectively disable automatic escalation.
Duration in minutes of the automatic timeout triggered when warn_threshold is reached and warn_escalation_action is 'timeout'.

Master switch for notifying members by DM.  When on, the actions listed in 'dm_actions' DM the affected member a notice (the action + reason) — when off, no moderation DM is ever sent.  Best-effort: silently skipped when the member has DMs closed.

Which actions DM the affected member when 'dm_on_action' is on: a comma-separated subset of warn, timeout, kick, ban (default all four).  Narrow it to suppress specific DMs — e.g. 'warn,timeout' to notify on warnings but not on a kick or ban.  Has no effect while the 'dm_on_action' master switch is off.
Custom body for the notify-the-member DM.  Leave empty for the built-in per-action notice.  Tokens: {guild} {action} {reason} {user}.
Require a non-empty reason for warn / kick / ban.  When on, the action is rejected (at the moderation_service seam) if no reason is given.  Timeout is exempt — its reason carries the duration.
Days of the banned member's recent messages to purge (0–7).  0 keeps all messages — today's default.
Upper bound (minutes) for any single timeout; longer requests are clamped down.  Default 40320 = 28 days, Discord's maximum, so an unconfigured guild is unaffected.
What happens when a member reaches warn_threshold warnings: 'timeout' (the default — auto-timeout for warn_timeout_minutes, then reset), 'kick', 'ban', or 'none' to disable auto-escalation.

After a kick or ban, sweep the member's recent messages in the channel where the action was taken: 'none' (default), 'kick', 'ban', or 'both'.  The bot needs Manage Messages + Read Message History in that channel.
How many recent messages to scan for the post-action cleanup sweep (1–500).  Only the moderated member's messages within that scan window are removed.
Channel for the optional public moderation log.  Leave empty to disable.  Public entries show the action, the affected member, and the reason — but never which moderator acted.

Which actions are announced in the public moderation log: 'none' (default, off), 'bans', 'removals' (kick + ban), or 'all' (warn + timeout + kick + ban).  The acting moderator is never shown publicly.  Requires public_log_channel to be set.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Role subsystem (cogs.role_cog).

keys.hint: When ON, members keep every tenure (time-based) role they have earned. When OFF (default), reaching a new tenure tier removes the previous one, so a member holds a single tenure role.
When ON (default), members keep every XP/level role they earn — the roles stack. When OFF, reaching a higher level role removes the lower level roles, so a member holds a single level role.

When ON (default), reacting on a tracked message self-assigns the bound role (the emoji reaction-role surface). Turn OFF to disable all emoji reaction roles for this server — the modern button / dropdown role menus are unaffected.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Security subsystem (cogs.security_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for server security (raid detection + account-age filter). When off, neither tier runs regardless of its own toggle. Off by default — a fresh server is unaffected.
Tier 1: watch the join rate and alert staff on a suspected raid.
Channel whose slowmode is raised during a raid lockdown. Leave unset for alert-only (no slowmode applied).
Tier 2: screen joining accounts younger than the threshold.
Action on a too-young account: 'alert' (staff alert only) or 'kick' (reject via moderation, with an alert).
Channel where staff security alerts (raid suspected / young account) are posted. Leave unset to run detection silently (events still fire).

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the Welcome subsystem (cogs.welcome_cog).

keys.hint: Master switch for welcome.  When off, no greeting posts and no entry role is granted regardless of the per-event toggles.  Off by default — a fresh server is unaffected.
Post a greeting embed in the welcome channel when a member joins.
Post a farewell embed in the welcome channel when a member leaves.
Channel where greeting/farewell embeds are posted.  Leave unset to disable posting (the entry role still works).
Greeting template.  Placeholders: {user} (mention), {server} (name), {count} (member number).  Add several variants separated by a '---' line to greet joiners with a random one each time.
Farewell template.  Placeholders: {user} (name), {server} (name), {count} (remaining members).  Add several variants separated by a '---' line to post a random one each time.
Role granted the moment a member joins (e.g. a base Member role).  Leave unset for none.  Granted through moderation's audited role path.
Also send the joining member a direct-message greeting (in addition to the channel greeting).  Off by default; needs no channel.  Silently skipped for members with DMs closed.
Direct-message greeting template.  Placeholders: {user} (mention), {server} (name), {count} (member number).  Add several variants separated by a '---' line to DM a random one each time.
keys.default: 👋 Welcome to **{server}**, {user}! Glad to have you — you're member #{count}.

keys.hint: Attach a rendered welcome card image to the join greeting (phase 2).  Off by default; falls back to the embed-only greeting when image rendering is unavailable.
Anti-raid: skip the greeting, DM, and entry role for a joining member whose Discord account is younger than this many days.  0 disables it (every account is greeted).
Ping-then-delete: auto-delete the channel greeting/farewell this many seconds after it posts, to keep a busy channel tidy.  0 keeps the message.  The DM greeting is never deleted.

settings.purpose: Settings keys owned by the XP subsystem (cogs.xp_cog).

keys.hint: Minimum XP awarded per qualifying message.
Maximum XP awarded per qualifying message.
Seconds between XP awards per user.  Zero disables the cooldown (not recommended in active guilds).
